subject: dogs
one_liner: Operational intelligence for the modern multi-dog household.
brand_voice_notes: Enterprise intelligence vendor that has somehow ended up in the pet category. Capabilities not features. Speaks about a Goldendoodle the way Palantir speaks about adversarial threats. Tone is institutional and slightly menacing. Pricing is confident. The bit is that this is real software people buy.

hero.sub: Houndsight ingests every behavior in your kennel, fuses it into a single ground-truth model, and delivers it back to you as auditable, defensible action. Used by 600+ private households and one zoo.

features.title: Behavioral Pattern Fusion
features.blurb: Cross-references fourteen sensor vendors (GPS, weight pads, microphone arrays, kibble-bowl IMUs) to determine whether your dog is operating within its established personality envelope. Excursions trigger a priority-three alert.

features.title: Adversarial Squirrel Modeling
features.blurb: A continuously-updated graph database of every squirrel observed within 500m of the household, weighted by historical engagement frequency. Every walk ships with an Escalation Likelihood score and three pre-cleared response options.

features.title: Pack Sentiment Forecasting
features.blurb: Natural-language ingestion of every "good boy" uttered in the home, attributed to the correct subject via on-device speaker diarization. Output is a weekly Affection Allocation Report, stratified by dog, with statistically significant deviations flagged.

pricing_tiers.name: Multi-Subject
pricing_tiers.tagline: Up to four dogs. Nightly reporting cadence.

pricing_tiers.name: Sovereign Pack
pricing_tiers.tagline: Unlimited subjects. On-prem. SOC 2 Type II.
perks: Reporting cadence set with your veterinary lead
SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, FedRAMP Moderate

testimonials.quote: We were operating in a fog. After Houndsight, I know within four seconds of waking which dog has urinated and where.
testimonials.name: Margaret Allers-Vance
testimonials.title: Chief of Staff to a Bernedoodle named Pip

testimonials.quote: I no longer make decisions about kibble without a quorum.
testimonials.name: Theodore Ngu
testimonials.title: Director of Procurement, Three-Dog Estate (NY / LA / Aspen)

testimonials.quote: Our morning standup now includes the dogs. Attendance is optional but tracked.
testimonials.name: Dr. Inez Cabrera-Holm, DVM
testimonials.title: Head of Canine Affairs, Cabrera-Holm Family Office

demo_screen_concept: The Operations Console for one subject: Pip, Bernedoodle, 4y, Subject ID K-04412. Dense desktop-style intelligence dashboard. Left column: subject profile card with live Behavioral Compliance Index meter and current status. Center: a chronological Event Feed (timestamped lines like 'Pip approached door (intent confidence 73%)', 'Pip vocalized — classified non-threat', 'Pip refused kibble — flagged for review'). Right column top: a Threat Map panel showing local squirrels as labeled pins with Escalation Likelihood scores, clickable to reveal a dossier. Right column bottom: a 7-day sentiment graph (verbal-affection events received) with a trendline and a deviation flag. Bottom strip: a Command Queue where the operator can issue actions (Reinforce, De-escalate, Initiate Walk Protocol, Authorize Treat). Interactive: clicking a squirrel opens a modal dossier; toggling Command Queue items posts a fake confirmation event into the feed; the compliance meter and event feed update on a 4-second tick using preloaded fixtures.

fonts.body: "IBM Plex Sans", -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Helvetica Neue", Arial, sans-serif

design_direction.motion_vocab: Subtle terminal reveals on hero load. Amber pulse on live-status dots. No decorative motion. Hover states are border-color shifts and inset accent bars, never transforms. Reduced-motion respected.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Top of page: a thin classified-document header strip in mono (OPERATIONAL // K9 INTELLIGENCE // CLASSIFIED FOR PARTNER USE) with a live status dot. Below: oversized typographic claim left-aligned. To its right or below, a faux-console panel rendering live-style event lines on the bg. Dense grid lines and tick-mark dividers throughout. Section labels are all-caps mono with a numeric index (01 / 02 / 03).
